Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.","type":"number"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"add_labels","description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 5 label(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"type":"object"}},{"name":"missing_tool","description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"noop","description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"}}]}}

2026-03-12T02:17:33.8734779Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T02:15:23.091782009Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":56996,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-03-12T02:17:33.8766862Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T02:15:23.295001635Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":56996,\"title\":\"[MPG Generator] HEAD operations should generate self-check methods on resource itself with Response return type\",\"body\":\"## Problem Description\\n\\nWhen migrating from AutoRest (Swagger) to the TypeSpec MPG generator, **HEAD operations** are generated differently, causing **breaking changes** for GA packages. Additionally, `ArmResourceActionAsync` with a typed response model generates a **renamed method** instead of preserving the original method name.\\n\\nThis was discovered during the `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter` migration from AutoRest to TypeSpec, requiring **7 custom backward-compatibility shim files** to preserve the 1.0.0 GA public API surface.\\n\\n## Issue 1: HEAD operations — Self-check vs Parent-check pattern\\n\\n### Expected Behavior (AutoRest/Swagger)\\n\\nIn the old AutoRest-generated code, HEAD operations were generated as **self-check methods on the resource itself**, returning `Response`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// On ApiCenterApiDefinitionResource itself\\npublic virtual async Task\\u0026gt; HeadAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\npublic virtual Response Head(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\n### Actual Behavior (TypeSpec MPG Generator)\\n\\nAfter migration, the TypeSpec MPG generator places HEAD operations on the **parent resource** with a child name parameter, returning `Response` (not `Response`):\\n\\n```csharp\\n// On ApiCenterApiVersionResource (parent) instead of ApiCenterApiDefinitionResource (self)\\npublic virtual async Task HeadAsync(string definitionName, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\npublic virtual Response Head(string definitionName, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\nThis is a **double breaking change**:\\n1. **Method placement**: Moved from the resource itself to the parent resource\\n2. **Return type**: Changed from `Response` to `Response`\\n\\n### TypeSpec Definition\\n\\nThe TypeSpec defines HEAD as a standard operation on the resource interface using `ResourceInstanceParameters`:\\n\\n```typespec\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface Apis {\\n  @doc(\\u0026#34;Checks if specified API exists.\\u0026#34;)\\n  @head\\n  head(\\n    ...ResourceInstanceParameters\\u0026gt;,\\n  ): OkResponse | ErrorResponse;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Swagger Definition\\n\\nThe Swagger defines HEAD on the resource\\u0026#39;s own URL path (e.g., `.../apis/{apiName}`):\\n\\n```json\\n\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/.../apis/{apiName}\\u0026#34;: {\\n  \\u0026#34;head\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;operationId\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Apis_Head\\u0026#34;,\\n    \\u0026#34;description\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Checks if specified API exists.\\u0026#34;,\\n    \\u0026#34;responses\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;200\\u0026#34;: { \\u0026#34;description\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;The request has succeeded.\\u0026#34; },\\n      \\u0026#34;default\\u0026#34;: { ... }\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### tspCodeModel.json (Intermediate)\\n\\nThe tspCodeModel.json shows the HEAD operations with the correct resource name but the generator interprets them as parent-check operations:\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;name\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;HeadApi\\u0026#34;,\\n  \\u0026#34;httpMethod\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;HEAD\\u0026#34;,\\n  \\u0026#34;resourceName\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Apis\\u0026#34;,\\n  \\u0026#34;responses\\u0026#34;: [{ \\u0026#34;statusCodes\\u0026#34;: [200], \\u0026#34;bodyType\\u0026#34;: null }]\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Affected Resources\\n\\nAll 7 resources with HEAD operations are affected:\\n\\n| Resource | Old (AutoRest) | New (TypeSpec MPG) |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `ApiCenterApiDefinitionResource` | `Head()` → `Response` on self | Not generated on self; `Head(string definitionName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterApiVersionResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterApiResource` | `Head()` → `Response` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadApi(string apiName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterWorkspaceResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterApiVersionResource` | `Head()` → `Response` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadApiVersion(string versionName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterApiResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterDeploymentResource` | `Head()` → `Response` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadDeployment(string deploymentName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterApiResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterEnvironmentResource` | `Head()` → `Response` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadEnvironment(string environmentName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterWorkspaceResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterMetadataSchemaResource` | `Head()` → `Response` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadMetadataSchema(string metadataSchemaName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterServiceResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterWorkspaceResource` | `Head()` → `Response` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadWorkspace(string workspaceName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterServiceResource` |\\n\\n## Issue 2: ArmResourceActionAsync with typed response renames the method\\n\\n### Expected Behavior (AutoRest/Swagger)\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic virtual async Task ImportSpecificationAsync(WaitUntil waitUntil, ApiSpecImportContent content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\npublic virtual ArmOperation ImportSpecification(WaitUntil waitUntil, ApiSpecImportContent content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\n### Actual Behavior (TypeSpec MPG Generator)\\n\\nThe method is **renamed** to `ImportSpecificationWithResult` and returns a typed `ArmOperation`:\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic virtual async Task\\u0026gt; ImportSpecificationWithResultAsync(WaitUntil waitUntil, ApiSpecImportContent content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\npublic virtual ArmOperation ImportSpecificationWithResult(WaitUntil waitUntil, ApiSpecImportContent content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\n### TypeSpec Definition\\n\\n```typespec\\n@doc(\\u0026#34;Imports the API specification.\\u0026#34;)\\nimportSpecification is ArmResourceActionAsync\\u0026lt;\\n  ApiDefinition,\\n  ApiSpecImportRequest,\\n  ApiImportSuccess\\n\\u0026gt;;\\n\\nmodel ApiImportSuccess {\\n  @statusCode code: 200;\\n}\\n```\\n\\nBecause the TypeSpec defines a typed response model (`ApiImportSuccess`), the generator appends `WithResult` to the method name and changes the return type from `ArmOperation` to `ArmOperation`.\\n\\n## Current Workaround\\n\\nAll 7 resource classes require manual custom backward-compatibility shim code in `src/Customization/` to preserve the 1.0.0 GA public API surface. These shims:\\n- Manually construct HTTP requests and process responses\\n- Convert HTTP status codes to `bool` for HEAD operations\\n- Delegate to the new generated methods for ImportSpecification\\n\\nSee: `sdk/apicenter/Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter/src/Customization/`\\n\\n## Suggested Fix\\n\\n1. **HEAD operations**: The generator should generate HEAD as a **self-check method on the resource itself** returning `Response` (matching AutoRest behavior), in addition to or instead of the parent-check pattern.\\n2. **ArmResourceActionAsync**: When the response model only contains a `@statusCode` field (like `ApiImportSuccess`), the generator should preserve the original method name and return non-generic `ArmOperation`, treating the response as effectively void.\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56996\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"mzhongl524\",\"id\":13110707,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/mzhongl524\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13110707?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-12T02:13:07Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-12T02:13:07Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-12T02:17:33.8799961Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T02:15:28.716796384Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"[]","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-03-12T02:17:33.8801525Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T02:15:28.766325493Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"color\":\"ffeb77\",\"description\":\"This issue is related to a management package.\",\"id\":\"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ4MTEx\",\"name\":\"Mgmt\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-03-12T02:17:33.8841446Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T02:15:28.98169141Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4061650320,\"number\":56996,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[MPG Generator] HEAD operations should generate self-check methods on resource itself with Response\\u003cbool\\u003e return type\",\"body\":\"## Problem Description\\n\\nWhen migrating from AutoRest (Swagger) to the TypeSpec MPG generator, **HEAD operations** are generated differently, causing **breaking changes** for GA packages. Additionally, `ArmResourceActionAsync` with a typed response model generates a **renamed method** instead of preserving the original method name.\\n\\nThis was discovered during the `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter` migration from AutoRest to TypeSpec, requiring **7 custom backward-compatibility shim files** to preserve the 1.0.0 GA public API surface.\\n\\n## Issue 1: HEAD operations — Self-check vs Parent-check pattern\\n\\n### Expected Behavior (AutoRest/Swagger)\\n\\nIn the old AutoRest-generated code, HEAD operations were generated as **self-check methods on the resource itself**, returning `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// On ApiCenterApiDefinitionResource itself\\npublic virtual async Task\\u003cResponse\\u003cbool\\u003e\\u003e HeadAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\npublic virtual Response\\u003cbool\\u003e Head(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\n### Actual Behavior (TypeSpec MPG Generator)\\n\\nAfter migration, the TypeSpec MPG generator places HEAD operations on the **parent resource** with a child name parameter, returning `Response` (not `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e`):\\n\\n```csharp\\n// On ApiCenterApiVersionResource (parent) instead of ApiCenterApiDefinitionResource (self)\\npublic virtual async Task\\u003cResponse\\u003e HeadAsync(string definitionName, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\npublic virtual Response Head(string definitionName, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\nThis is a **double breaking change**:\\n1. **Method placement**: Moved from the resource itself to the parent resource\\n2. **Return type**: Changed from `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` to `Response`\\n\\n### TypeSpec Definition\\n\\nThe TypeSpec defines HEAD as a standard operation on the resource interface using `ResourceInstanceParameters`:\\n\\n```typespec\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface Apis {\\n  @doc(\\\"Checks if specified API exists.\\\")\\n  @head\\n  head(\\n    ...ResourceInstanceParameters\\u003cApi, Foundations.BaseParameters\\u003cApi\\u003e\\u003e,\\n  ): OkResponse | ErrorResponse;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Swagger Definition\\n\\nThe Swagger defines HEAD on the resource's own URL path (e.g., `.../apis/{apiName}`):\\n\\n```json\\n\\\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/.../apis/{apiName}\\\": {\\n  \\\"head\\\": {\\n    \\\"operationId\\\": \\\"Apis_Head\\\",\\n    \\\"description\\\": \\\"Checks if specified API exists.\\\",\\n    \\\"responses\\\": {\\n      \\\"200\\\": { \\\"description\\\": \\\"The request has succeeded.\\\" },\\n      \\\"default\\\": { ... }\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### tspCodeModel.json (Intermediate)\\n\\nThe tspCodeModel.json shows the HEAD operations with the correct resource name but the generator interprets them as parent-check operations:\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"name\\\": \\\"HeadApi\\\",\\n  \\\"httpMethod\\\": \\\"HEAD\\\",\\n  \\\"resourceName\\\": \\\"Apis\\\",\\n  \\\"responses\\\": [{ \\\"statusCodes\\\": [200], \\\"bodyType\\\": null }]\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Affected Resources\\n\\nAll 7 resources with HEAD operations are affected:\\n\\n| Resource | Old (AutoRest) | New (TypeSpec MPG) |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `ApiCenterApiDefinitionResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `Head(string definitionName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterApiVersionResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterApiResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadApi(string apiName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterWorkspaceResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterApiVersionResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadApiVersion(string versionName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterApiResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterDeploymentResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadDeployment(string deploymentName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterApiResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterEnvironmentResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadEnvironment(string environmentName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterWorkspaceResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterMetadataSchemaResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadMetadataSchema(string metadataSchemaName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterServiceResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterWorkspaceResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadWorkspace(string workspaceName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterServiceResource` |\\n\\n## Issue 2: ArmResourceActionAsync with typed response renames the method\\n\\n### Expected Behavior (AutoRest/Swagger)\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic virtual async Task\\u003cArmOperation\\u003e ImportSpecificationAsync(WaitUntil waitUntil, ApiSpecImportContent content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\npublic virtual ArmOperation ImportSpecification(WaitUntil waitUntil, ApiSpecImportContent content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\n### Actual Behavior (TypeSpec MPG Generator)\\n\\nThe method is **renamed** to `ImportSpecificationWithResult` and returns a typed `ArmOperation\\u003cT\\u003e`:\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic virtual async Task\\u003cArmOperation\\u003cApiCenterApiImportSuccess\\u003e\\u003e ImportSpecificationWithResultAsync(WaitUntil waitUntil, ApiSpecImportContent content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\npublic virtual ArmOperation\\u003cApiCenterApiImportSuccess\\u003e ImportSpecificationWithResult(WaitUntil waitUntil, ApiSpecImportContent content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\n### TypeSpec Definition\\n\\n```typespec\\n@doc(\\\"Imports the API specification.\\\")\\nimportSpecification is ArmResourceActionAsync\\u003c\\n  ApiDefinition,\\n  ApiSpecImportRequest,\\n  ApiImportSuccess\\n\\u003e;\\n\\nmodel ApiImportSuccess {\\n  @statusCode code: 200;\\n}\\n```\\n\\nBecause the TypeSpec defines a typed response model (`ApiImportSuccess`), the generator appends `WithResult` to the method name and changes the return type from `ArmOperation` to `ArmOperation\\u003cApiCenterApiImportSuccess\\u003e`.\\n\\n## Current Workaround\\n\\nAll 7 resource classes require manual custom backward-compatibility shim code in `src/Customization/` to preserve the 1.0.0 GA public API surface. These shims:\\n- Manually construct HTTP requests and process responses\\n- Convert HTTP status codes to `bool` for HEAD operations\\n- Delegate to the new generated methods for ImportSpecification\\n\\nSee: `sdk/apicenter/Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter/src/Customization/`\\n\\n## Suggested Fix\\n\\n1. **HEAD operations**: The generator should generate HEAD as a **self-check method on the resource itself** returning `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` (matching AutoRest behavior), in addition to or instead of the parent-check pattern.\\n2. **ArmResourceActionAsync**: When the response model only contains a `@statusCode` field (like `ApiImportSuccess`), the generator should preserve the original method name and return non-generic `ArmOperation`, treating the response as effectively void.\\r\\n\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"mzhongl524\",\"id\":13110707,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjEzMTEwNzA3\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13110707?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/mzhongl524\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/mzhongl524\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/mzhongl524/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/mzhongl524/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/mzhongl524/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/mzhongl524/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/mzhongl524/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/mzhongl524/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/mzhongl524/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/mzhongl524/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/mzhongl524/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1369687458,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/bug\",\"name\":\"bug\",\"color\":\"eaa875\",\"description\":\"This issue requires a change to an existing behavior in the product in order to be resolved.\",\"default\":true,\"node_id\":\"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzY5Njg3NDU4\"}],\"comments\":0,\"created_at\":\"2026-03-12T02:13:07Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-12T02:13:07Z\",\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56996\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56996\",\"comments_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56996/comments\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56996/events\",\"labels_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56996/labels{/name}\",\"repository_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56996/reactions\"},\"node_id\":\"I_kwDOACyxMM7yF92Q\"}]}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-12T02:17:33.8931273Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T02:15:43.036229562Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4061650320,\"number\":56996,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[MPG Generator] HEAD operations should generate self-check methods on resource itself with Response\\u003cbool\\u003e return type\",\"body\":\"## Problem Description\\n\\nWhen migrating from AutoRest (Swagger) to the TypeSpec MPG generator, **HEAD operations** are generated differently, causing **breaking changes** for GA packages. Additionally, `ArmResourceActionAsync` with a typed response model generates a **renamed method** instead of preserving the original method name.\\n\\nThis was discovered during the `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter` migration from AutoRest to TypeSpec, requiring **7 custom backward-compatibility shim files** to preserve the 1.0.0 GA public API surface.\\n\\n## Issue 1: HEAD operations — Self-check vs Parent-check pattern\\n\\n### Expected Behavior (AutoRest/Swagger)\\n\\nIn the old AutoRest-generated code, HEAD operations were generated as **self-check methods on the resource itself**, returning `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// On ApiCenterApiDefinitionResource itself\\npublic virtual async Task\\u003cResponse\\u003cbool\\u003e\\u003e HeadAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\npublic virtual Response\\u003cbool\\u003e Head(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\n### Actual Behavior (TypeSpec MPG Generator)\\n\\nAfter migration, the TypeSpec MPG generator places HEAD operations on the **parent resource** with a child name parameter, returning `Response` (not `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e`):\\n\\n```csharp\\n// On ApiCenterApiVersionResource (parent) instead of ApiCenterApiDefinitionResource (self)\\npublic virtual async Task\\u003cResponse\\u003e HeadAsync(string definitionName, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\npublic virtual Response Head(string definitionName, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\nThis is a **double breaking change**:\\n1. **Method placement**: Moved from the resource itself to the parent resource\\n2. **Return type**: Changed from `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` to `Response`\\n\\n### TypeSpec Definition\\n\\nThe TypeSpec defines HEAD as a standard operation on the resource interface using `ResourceInstanceParameters`:\\n\\n```typespec\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface Apis {\\n  @doc(\\\"Checks if specified API exists.\\\")\\n  @head\\n  head(\\n    ...ResourceInstanceParameters\\u003cApi, Foundations.BaseParameters\\u003cApi\\u003e\\u003e,\\n  ): OkResponse | ErrorResponse;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Swagger Definition\\n\\nThe Swagger defines HEAD on the resource's own URL path (e.g., `.../apis/{apiName}`):\\n\\n```json\\n\\\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/.../apis/{apiName}\\\": {\\n  \\\"head\\\": {\\n    \\\"operationId\\\": \\\"Apis_Head\\\",\\n    \\\"description\\\": \\\"Checks if specified API exists.\\\",\\n    \\\"responses\\\": {\\n      \\\"200\\\": { \\\"description\\\": \\\"The request has succeeded.\\\" },\\n      \\\"default\\\": { ... }\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### tspCodeModel.json (Intermediate)\\n\\nThe tspCodeModel.json shows the HEAD operations with the correct resource name but the generator interprets them as parent-check operations:\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"name\\\": \\\"HeadApi\\\",\\n  \\\"httpMethod\\\": \\\"HEAD\\\",\\n  \\\"resourceName\\\": \\\"Apis\\\",\\n  \\\"responses\\\": [{ \\\"statusCodes\\\": [200], \\\"bodyType\\\": null }]\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Affected Resources\\n\\nAll 7 resources with HEAD operations are affected:\\n\\n| Resource | Old (AutoRest) | New (TypeSpec MPG) |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `ApiCenterApiDefinitionResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `Head(string definitionName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterApiVersionResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterApiResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadApi(string apiName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterWorkspaceResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterApiVersionResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadApiVersion(string versionName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterApiResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterDeploymentResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadDeployment(string deploymentName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterApiResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterEnvironmentResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadEnvironment(string environmentName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterWorkspaceResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterMetadataSchemaResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadMetadataSchema(string metadataSchemaName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterServiceResource` |\\n| `ApiCenterWorkspaceResource` | `Head()` → `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` on self | Not generated on self; `HeadWorkspace(string workspaceName)` → `Response` on `ApiCenterServiceResource` |\\n\\n## Issue 2: ArmResourceActionAsync with typed response renames the method\\n\\n### Expected Behavior (AutoRest/Swagger)\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic virtual async Task\\u003cArmOperation\\u003e ImportSpecificationAsync(WaitUntil waitUntil, ApiSpecImportContent content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\npublic virtual ArmOperation ImportSpecification(WaitUntil waitUntil, ApiSpecImportContent content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\n### Actual Behavior (TypeSpec MPG Generator)\\n\\nThe method is **renamed** to `ImportSpecificationWithResult` and returns a typed `ArmOperation\\u003cT\\u003e`:\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic virtual async Task\\u003cArmOperation\\u003cApiCenterApiImportSuccess\\u003e\\u003e ImportSpecificationWithResultAsync(WaitUntil waitUntil, ApiSpecImportContent content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\npublic virtual ArmOperation\\u003cApiCenterApiImportSuccess\\u003e ImportSpecificationWithResult(WaitUntil waitUntil, ApiSpecImportContent content, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\n### TypeSpec Definition\\n\\n```typespec\\n@doc(\\\"Imports the API specification.\\\")\\nimportSpecification is ArmResourceActionAsync\\u003c\\n  ApiDefinition,\\n  ApiSpecImportRequest,\\n  ApiImportSuccess\\n\\u003e;\\n\\nmodel ApiImportSuccess {\\n  @statusCode code: 200;\\n}\\n```\\n\\nBecause the TypeSpec defines a typed response model (`ApiImportSuccess`), the generator appends `WithResult` to the method name and changes the return type from `ArmOperation` to `ArmOperation\\u003cApiCenterApiImportSuccess\\u003e`.\\n\\n## Current Workaround\\n\\nAll 7 resource classes require manual custom backward-compatibility shim code in `src/Customization/` to preserve the 1.0.0 GA public API surface. These shims:\\n- Manually construct HTTP requests and process responses\\n- Convert HTTP status codes to `bool` for HEAD operations\\n- Delegate to the new generated methods for ImportSpecification\\n\\nSee: `sdk/apicenter/Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter/src/Customization/`\\n\\n## Suggested Fix\\n\\n1. **HEAD operations**: The generator should generate HEAD as a **self-check method on the resource itself** returning `Response\\u003cbool\\u003e` (matching AutoRest behavior), in addition to or instead of the parent-check pattern.\\n2. **ArmResourceActionAsync**: When the response model only contains a `@statusCode` field (like `ApiImportSuccess`), the generator should preserve the original method name and return non-generic `ArmOperation`, treating the response as effectively 2026-03-12T02:17:33.8997706Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T02:16:50.353076946Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\nThis is a well-documented bug report covering **two generator-level breaking changes** in the TypeSpec MPG (Management Plane Generator) that affect the `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter` migration from AutoRest to TypeSpec. The author is an Azure SDK team member. No spam or bot content detected.\n\n**Summary**: The MPG generator produces HEAD operations on the *parent* resource with a child name parameter and `Response` return type, instead of self-check methods on the resource itself with `Response\u003cbool\u003e`. Additionally, `ArmResourceActionAsync` with a status-code-only typed response appends `WithResult` to the method name unnecessarily.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Breakdown\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n### Issue 1 — HEAD operations: Self-check vs. Parent-check pattern\n- **Root cause**: The MPG generator interprets HEAD operations as parent-scoped existence checks, even when the TypeSpec defines them via `ResourceInstanceParameters` on the resource's own interface.\n- **Breaking changes introduced**:\n  1. Method moves from the resource itself to the parent resource\n  2. Return type changes from `Response\u003cbool\u003e` to `Response`\n- **Affected resources**: 7 resources in `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter` (see issue table)\n- **Generator entry point to investigate**: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` — specifically the code path that handles `httpMethod == \"HEAD\"` and decides where to place the method.\n\n### Issue 2 — `ArmResourceActionAsync` method rename with typed response\n- **Root cause**: When a TypeSpec model containing only a `@statusCode` field is used as the response type for `ArmResourceActionAsync`, the generator appends `WithResult` to the method name and changes the return type from `ArmOperation` to `ArmOperation\u003cT\u003e`.\n- **Proposed fix**: Detect when the response model contains only a `@statusCode` and no meaningful body, and treat it as void — preserving the original method name and non-generic `ArmOperation` return type.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔍 Debugging Strategy\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n1. **Locate HEAD handling logic** in the generator:\n   - File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/` — search for HEAD HTTP method handling or existence check method generation.\n   - Compare how AutoRest processed `Apis_Head` operationIds vs. how the TypeSpec emitter maps `resourceName` + `httpMethod: HEAD`.\n\n2. **Inspect tspCodeModel.json output**: The intermediate JSON shows `\"resourceName\": \"Apis\"` and `\"httpMethod\": \"HEAD\"` — verify whether the emitter correctly sets the resource context or incorrectly uses a parent scope.\n\n3. **ArmResourceActionAsync `WithResult` logic**:\n   - Search for the `WithResult` suffix logic in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/`.\n   - Add a condition: if the response model only contains `@statusCode` (no body fields), treat it as non-generic.\n\n4. **Test with ApiCenter customization removed**: Temporarily remove one of the 7 shim files from `sdk/apicenter/Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter/src/Customization/` and regenerate to observe the generator output directly.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Suggested Sub-Tasks\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Investigate HEAD operation placement logic in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/` and identify where self-check vs. parent-check is decided\n- [ ] Fix HEAD operations to generate `Head()`/`HeadAsync()` → `Response\u003cbool\u003e` on the resource itself (matching AutoRest behavior)\n- [ ] Investigate `WithResult` suffix logic for `ArmResourceActionAsync` in the generator\n- [ ] Fix: when the response model contains only `@statusCode` with no body, do not append `WithResult` and return non-generic `ArmOperation`\n- [ ] Run `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` to validate generator changes\n- [ ] Validate `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter` regenerates correctly and the 7 customization shim files can be removed\n- [ ] Add regression tests in the generator test suite for HEAD and `ArmResourceActionAsync` patterns\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔗 Relevant Resources\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt) — MPG generator source\n- [`sdk/apicenter/Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter/src/Customization/`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/apicenter/Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter/src/Customization) — existing workaround shims\n- [CODEOWNERS for MPG generator](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/CODEOWNERS): `@JoshLove-msft @m-nash @jorgerangel-msft @live1206 @ArcturusZhang`\n- [CODEOWNERS for ApiCenter SDK](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/CODEOWNERS): `@ArcturusZhang @ArthurMa1978`\n- [Azure SDK Breaking Change Rules](https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/docs/coding-guidelines/breaking-change-rules.md)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n","item_number":56996},"name":"add_comment"}}}

2026-03-12T02:17:33.9033753Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T02:17:00.756039944Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\nThis is a well-documented bug report covering **two generator-level breaking changes** in the TypeSpec MPG (Management Plane Generator) that affect the `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter` migration from AutoRest to TypeSpec. The author is an Azure SDK team member.\n\n**Summary**: The MPG generator produces HEAD operations on the *parent* resource with a child name parameter and `Response` return type, instead of self-check methods on the resource itself with `Response\u003cbool\u003e`. Additionally, `ArmResourceActionAsync` with a status-code-only typed response appends `WithResult` to the method name unnecessarily.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Breakdown\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n### Issue 1 — HEAD operations: Self-check vs. Parent-check pattern\n- **Root cause**: The MPG generator interprets HEAD operations as parent-scoped existence checks, even when the TypeSpec defines them via `ResourceInstanceParameters` on the resource's own interface.\n- **Breaking changes introduced**:\n  1. Method moves from the resource itself to the parent resource\n  2. Return type changes from `Response\u003cbool\u003e` to `Response`\n- **Affected resources**: 7 resources in `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter` (see issue table)\n- **Generator entry point to investigate**: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` — specifically the code path that handles `httpMethod == \"HEAD\"` and decides where to place the method.\n\n### Issue 2 — `ArmResourceActionAsync` method rename with typed response\n- **Root cause**: When a TypeSpec model containing only a `@statusCode` field is used as the response type for `ArmResourceActionAsync`, the generator appends `WithResult` to the method name and changes the return type from `ArmOperation` to `ArmOperation\u003cT\u003e`.\n- **Proposed fix**: Detect when the response model contains only a `@statusCode` and no meaningful body, and treat it as void — preserving the original method name and non-generic `ArmOperation` return type.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔍 Debugging Strategy\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n1. **Locate HEAD handling logic** in the generator:\n   - File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/` — search for HEAD HTTP method handling or existence check method generation.\n   - Compare how AutoRest processed `Apis_Head` operationIds vs. how the TypeSpec emitter maps `resourceName` + `httpMethod: HEAD`.\n\n2. **Inspect tspCodeModel.json output**: The intermediate JSON shows `\"resourceName\": \"Apis\"` and `\"httpMethod\": \"HEAD\"` — verify whether the emitter correctly sets the resource context or incorrectly uses a parent scope.\n\n3. **ArmResourceActionAsync `WithResult` logic**:\n   - Search for the `WithResult` suffix logic in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/`.\n   - Add a condition: if the response model only contains `@statusCode` (no body fields), treat it as non-generic.\n\n4. **Test with ApiCenter customization removed**: Temporarily remove one of the 7 shim files from `sdk/apicenter/Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter/src/Customization/` and regenerate to observe the generator output directly.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Suggested Sub-Tasks\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Investigate HEAD operation placement logic in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/` and identify where self-check vs. parent-check is decided\n- [ ] Fix HEAD operations to generate `Head()`/`HeadAsync()` → `Response\u003cbool\u003e` on the resource itself (matching AutoRest behavior)\n- [ ] Investigate `WithResult` suffix logic for `ArmResourceActionAsync` in the generator\n- [ ] Fix: when the response model contains

2026-03-12T02:17:33.9400325Z       },
